content.subtitle: Use
rows: We use **so ... that** and **such ... that** to express a cause and its result — the first part explains the reason, and the second part shows the consequence.
The film was **so scary** that I couldn't sleep.

content.subtitle: Form
rows: so + adjective + that
The weather was **so hot** that we stayed indoors all day.
so + many/much + noun + that
She has **so many friends** that she never feels lonely.
such + a/an + adjective + noun + that
It was **such a long journey** that we stopped twice.
such + adjective + plural noun + that
They gave **such interesting answers** that the teacher was impressed.
such + a lot of + noun + that
He drinks **such a lot of coffee** that he can't sleep at night.
so + adverb + that
She sings **so beautifully** that everyone stopped to listen.

content.subtitle: Use
rows: We use **too** to say that something is more than necessary or acceptable, with a negative effect.
The coffee is **too hot** to drink right now.
We use **enough** to say that there is the right amount of something, or to say there is not enough.
She isn't **old enough** to get a driving licence.

content.subtitle: Form
rows: too + adjective (+ full infinitive)
The suitcase is **too heavy** to carry.
adjective + enough (+ full infinitive)
Are you **fit enough** to run a marathon?
too + many/much + noun (+ full infinitive)
There are **too many people** in this room to have a quiet conversation.
enough + noun (+ full infinitive)
We have **enough time** to grab a coffee before the meeting.
too + adverb (+ full infinitive)
He spoke **too quickly** for me to take notes.
adverb + enough (+ full infinitive)
She performed **well enough** to get a place in the final.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: **Too** is not the same as **very**. We only use **too** when something causes a problem.
**Enough** always follows the adjective or adverb: I'm not **confident enough** to speak in public.
Both **too** and **enough** can be followed by **for + noun/pronoun**: It's too loud **for** the baby to sleep.
